data.name: Venue Visualizer
data.description: Generate a venue visualizer webpage — three 16:9 real venue shots, each with a crossfade toggle that fades a brand activation on and off within the real space. Final output is a self-contained interactive HTML page published via PublishWebpage. Works for any event brand, any venue.
data.documentation:
# Venue Visualizer **Purpose**: Show what a brand looks like *activated inside a real venue* — three 16:9 shots, each with a smooth crossfade toggle between the empty venue and the brand-activated version. Final output is a self-contained interactive HTML page (PublishWebpage). --- ## Inputs needed from the user | Input | Notes | |---|---| | 3–5 venue photos | Real photography of the venue — interior, courtyard, entrance, etc. Wider / landscape preferred but any format works; the crop step normalizes to 16:9 | | Brand logo / asset | The actual logo file. Feeding the real asset to GPT Image 2 as an `inputImages` reference is the critical step for logo fidelity | | Brand name | String used in the page title, toggle button, and activation prompt | | Brand accent colors | 1–3 hex values. Defaults: `#b9ee46` (lime), `#bcaaff` (lavender), `#8fd2ff` (sky) | | Shot labels | 3 short names, e.g. "Arrival Lobby", "The Courtyard", "The Entrance" | | Short captions | One line per shot describing what the activation is, e.g. "Becomes the brand booth" | --- ## Workflow (step by step) ### Step 1 — Prepare blank-slate before-plates Crop each venue photo to exact **16:9 at 1536×864** using the provided crop script. This ensures before/after images are pixel-matched, making the crossfade seamless. ```bash bash skills/venue-visualizer/crop_venue_shots.sh venue1.jpg venue2.jpg venue3.jpg -o workspace/ ``` The ffmpeg filter used: ``` crop=iw:trunc(iw*9/16/2)*2,scale=1536:864 ``` **Host each cropped blank** with `SaveFile` + `PublishFilePublicly` so GPT Image 2 can receive them as `inputImages` URLs. Thread-scoped `/api/files/...` URLs work as `inputImages` even without publishing, but publish them now anyway — you'll need the public URLs for the HTML. --- ### Step 2 — Host the brand asset Save and publish the user's logo/brand file with `SaveFile` + `PublishFilePublicly`. You'll pass this as the second `inputImages` entry on every generation. --- ### Step 3 — Generate activated versions (GPT Image 2) Generate one activated image per venue shot. Use this prompt template: ``` IMAGE 1 is a real photo of [VENUE NAME] — preserve its architecture, [describe key features: wood/greenery/columns/etc], camera angle and lighting EXACTLY. IMAGE 2 is the [BRAND NAME] event branding — reproduce the [describe wordmark] "[BRAND NAME]" wordmark EXACTLY. [No other text, no dates.] Keep the room EXACTLY as in IMAGE 1 — identical architecture, walls, floor, plants, camera angle and lighting. Do NOT move or change the space. ONLY add a tasteful [BRAND NAME] brand-activation [SURFACE TYPE: booth / lounge / registration desk] into the existing scene: - A large "[BRAND NAME]" [wordmark description] sign mounted prominently and centered - [2–3 specific activation elements: demo screens / counter / lounge seating / photo backdrop] - Soft [accent colors] pastel accent lighting that ties to the brand Warm, premium, photorealistic, full color, widescreen. [No crowds / A few casual attendees]. The only text anywhere is the "[BRAND NAME]" wordmark — crisp and legible. ``` **Key parameters:** - `model: "openai/gpt-image-2"` — always use GPT Image 2 for logo-faithful typography - `aspectRatio: "16:9"` — must match the blank plates - `quality: "high"` - `inputImages: [blank_plate_url, brand_logo_url]` — two images, always in this order **Critical learnings:** - "Keep the room EXACTLY as in IMAGE 1" is the most important line — without it the model reimagines the space - Always pass the real brand asset as `inputImages[1]`; text description alone causes logo drift - "No fine print, no dates, only the [BRAND NAME] wordmark" prevents hallucinated event details - "No crowds" keeps the toggle readable - Never ask for colors in a logo that should be monochrome — say "strictly monochrome black, NO red/yellow/blue" explicitly --- ### Step 4 — QA each before/after pair ```bash for n in 1 2 3; do ffmpeg -y -i blank${n}.png -i act${n}.png \ -filter_complex "[0]scale=760:-1[a];[1]scale=760:-1[b];[a][b]vstack" pair${n}.png 2>/dev/null done ffmpeg -y -i pair1.png -i pair2.png -i pair3.png \ -filter_complex "[0]scale=-1:760[a];[1]scale=-1:760[b];[2]scale=-1:760[c];[a][b][c]hstack=3" qa_grid.png 2>/dev/null ``` Use `Read` to visually inspect. Accept if: room architecture is identical, wordmark is legible and faithful, no unintended text. If a panel fails, regenerate with "Do NOT add, remove, or modify ANY architectural element." --- ### Step 5 — Publish all 6 images `PublishFilePublicly` for each blank + activated image. The iframe served by PublishWebpage is sandboxed — thread-scoped `/api/files/...` URLs will be blocked. All `` values in the HTML **must** be `pub.hyperagent.com` URLs. --- ### Step 6 — Generate the HTML ```bash python3 skills/venue-visualizer/generate_visualizer.py \ --blank BLANK1_URL BLANK2_URL BLANK3_URL \ --act ACT1_URL ACT2_URL ACT3_URL \ --brand "AIRSPACE" \ --venue "1 Hotel West Hollywood" \ --labels "Arrival Lobby" "The Courtyard" "The Entrance" \ --captions \ "Becomes the brand booth — hero signage, demo screens, welcome counter." \ "Becomes the Build Bar — overhead signage, communal counter, lounge." \ "Becomes registration — branded wall, desk, photo moment." \ --accent1 "#b9ee46" --accent2 "#bcaaff" --accent3 "#8fd2ff" \ --font "Pixelify Sans" \ --output workspace/visualizer.html ``` --- ### Step 7 — Publish the page ```python PublishWebpage(filePath="workspace/visualizer.html", title="BRAND — Venue Visualizer") ``` --- ## Supported variations | Variation | How | |---|---| | Portrait triptych | Use `aspectRatio: "3:4"` in generation; crop with `crop=trunc(ih*3/4/2)*2:ih` | | Dark-mode page | Change `--paper` to `#0a0a0c` and `--ink` to `#f3f2ee` in the generated HTML | | More / fewer shots | The script generates one `
` per pair passed | | Sponsor logo in header | Embed a `` beneath `

` using the published logo URL | --- ## Activation surface prompts (ready to use) **Exhibition booth:** > "A sleek brand-activation booth — a large [BRAND] sign on the wall, two demo screens on slim stands showing clean grid interfaces, a low welcome counter, soft [ACCENT] pastel accents. No crowds." **Build Bar / communal lounge:** > "A [BRAND] Build Bar — a large [BRAND] sign overhead, communal counter with bar stools, warm pendant lights, lounge chairs and plants, soft [ACCENT] accents. A few effortlessly cool attendees in elevated casual (linen, denim) collaborate at the counter." **Registration + photo-op:** > "A [BRAND] registration moment — a large [BRAND] backdrop wall to one side, a sleek check-in desk, badge/lanyard stand. No hanging ceiling banners. The central entrance is completely unobstructed." **Keynote stage:** > "A [BRAND]-presented keynote — large LED backdrop displaying the [BRAND] logo and '[EVENT]' in the existing stage architecture. Keep all stage lighting, the podium, and audience seating. A speaker stands at the podium." --- ## Common failure modes & fixes | Failure | Fix | |---|---| | Room architecture changes | Add "Do NOT add, remove, or modify ANY architectural element — every wall, floor, column, window, plant and fixture must be unchanged" | | Logo has wrong colors | Add "Strictly [COLOR], absolutely NO [OTHER COLORS]" + pass real logo as `inputImages[1]` | | Fine print / dates appear | Add "The ONLY text anywhere is the [BRAND] wordmark. No dates, no city names, no taglines." | | Doorway blocked by booth | Add "Keep the central entrance completely unobstructed. Place all activation elements flanking the sides only." | | People look corporate | Add "NO business suits. Elevated casual only — linen, quality denim, knitwear." |
data.whenToUse: Use when a user wants to show a sponsor, event producer, or brand partner what their brand would look like activated inside a real venue. Typical inputs: venue photos (any format) + a brand logo/asset. Output: an interactive toggle page where each shot shows the venue before/after brand activation.
data.skillMdBody:
# Venue Visualizer **Purpose**: Show what a brand looks like *activated inside a real venue* — three 16:9 shots, each with a smooth crossfade toggle between the empty venue and the brand-activated version. Final output is a self-contained interactive HTML page (PublishWebpage). --- ## Inputs needed from the user | Input | Notes | |---|---| | 3–5 venue photos | Real photography of the venue — interior, courtyard, entrance, etc. Wider / landscape preferred but any format works; the crop step normalizes to 16:9 | | Brand logo / asset | The actual logo file. Feeding the real asset to GPT Image 2 as an `inputImages` reference is the critical step for logo fidelity | | Brand name | String used in the page title, toggle button, and activation prompt | | Brand accent colors | 1–3 hex values. Defaults: `#b9ee46` (lime), `#bcaaff` (lavender), `#8fd2ff` (sky) | | Shot labels | 3 short names, e.g. "Arrival Lobby", "The Courtyard", "The Entrance" | | Short captions | One line per shot describing what the activation is, e.g. "Becomes the brand booth" | --- ## Workflow (step by step) ### Step 1 — Prepare blank-slate before-plates Crop each venue photo to exact **16:9 at 1536×864** using the provided crop script. This ensures before/after images are pixel-matched, making the crossfade seamless. ```bash bash skills/venue-visualizer/crop_venue_shots.sh venue1.jpg venue2.jpg venue3.jpg -o workspace/ ``` The ffmpeg filter used: ``` crop=iw:trunc(iw*9/16/2)*2,scale=1536:864 ``` **Host each cropped blank** with `SaveFile` + `PublishFilePublicly` so GPT Image 2 can receive them as `inputImages` URLs. Thread-scoped `/api/files/...` URLs work as `inputImages` even without publishing, but publish them now anyway — you'll need the public URLs for the HTML. --- ### Step 2 — Host the brand asset Save and publish the user's logo/brand file with `SaveFile` + `PublishFilePublicly`. You'll pass this as the second `inputImages` entry on every generation. --- ### Step 3 — Generate activated versions (GPT Image 2) Generate one activated image per venue shot. Use this prompt template: ``` IMAGE 1 is a real photo of [VENUE NAME] — preserve its architecture, [describe key features: wood/greenery/columns/etc], camera angle and lighting EXACTLY. IMAGE 2 is the [BRAND NAME] event branding — reproduce the [describe wordmark] "[BRAND NAME]" wordmark EXACTLY. [No other text, no dates.] Keep the room EXACTLY as in IMAGE 1 — identical architecture, walls, floor, plants, camera angle and lighting. Do NOT move or change the space. ONLY add a tasteful [BRAND NAME] brand-activation [SURFACE TYPE: booth / lounge / registration desk] into the existing scene: - A large "[BRAND NAME]" [wordmark description] sign mounted prominently and centered - [2–3 specific activation elements: demo screens / counter / lounge seating / photo backdrop] - Soft [accent colors] pastel accent lighting that ties to the brand Warm, premium, photorealistic, full color, widescreen. [No crowds / A few casual attendees]. The only text anywhere is the "[BRAND NAME]" wordmark — crisp and legible. ``` **Key parameters:** - `model: "openai/gpt-image-2"` — always use GPT Image 2 for logo-faithful typography - `aspectRatio: "16:9"` — must match the blank plates - `quality: "high"` - `inputImages: [blank_plate_url, brand_logo_url]` — two images, always in this order **Critical learnings:** - "Keep the room EXACTLY as in IMAGE 1" is the most important line — without it the model reimagines the space - Always pass the real brand asset as `inputImages[1]`; text description alone causes logo drift - "No fine print, no dates, only the [BRAND NAME] wordmark" prevents hallucinated event details - "No crowds" keeps the toggle readable - Never ask for colors in a logo that should be monochrome — say "strictly monochrome black, NO red/yellow/blue" explicitly --- ### Step 4 — QA each before/after pair ```bash for n in 1 2 3; do ffmpeg -y -i blank${n}.png -i act${n}.png \ -filter_complex "[0]scale=760:-1[a];[1]scale=760:-1[b];[a][b]vstack" pair${n}.png 2>/dev/null done ffmpeg -y -i pair1.png -i pair2.png -i pair3.png \ -filter_complex "[0]scale=-1:760[a];[1]scale=-1:760[b];[2]scale=-1:760[c];[a][b][c]hstack=3" qa_grid.png 2>/dev/null ``` Use `Read` to visually inspect. Accept if: room architecture is identical, wordmark is legible and faithful, no unintended text. If a panel fails, regenerate with "Do NOT add, remove, or modify ANY architectural element." --- ### Step 5 — Publish all 6 images `PublishFilePublicly` for each blank + activated image. The iframe served by PublishWebpage is sandboxed — thread-scoped `/api/files/...` URLs will be blocked. All `` values in the HTML **must** be `pub.hyperagent.com` URLs. --- ### Step 6 — Generate the HTML ```bash python3 skills/venue-visualizer/generate_visualizer.py \ --blank BLANK1_URL BLANK2_URL BLANK3_URL \ --act ACT1_URL ACT2_URL ACT3_URL \ --brand "AIRSPACE" \ --venue "1 Hotel West Hollywood" \ --labels "Arrival Lobby" "The Courtyard" "The Entrance" \ --captions \ "Becomes the brand booth — hero signage, demo screens, welcome counter." \ "Becomes the Build Bar — overhead signage, communal counter, lounge." \ "Becomes registration — branded wall, desk, photo moment." \ --accent1 "#b9ee46" --accent2 "#bcaaff" --accent3 "#8fd2ff" \ --font "Pixelify Sans" \ --output workspace/visualizer.html ``` --- ### Step 7 — Publish the page ```python PublishWebpage(filePath="workspace/visualizer.html", title="BRAND — Venue Visualizer") ``` --- ## Supported variations | Variation | How | |---|---| | Portrait triptych | Use `aspectRatio: "3:4"` in generation; crop with `crop=trunc(ih*3/4/2)*2:ih` | | Dark-mode page | Change `--paper` to `#0a0a0c` and `--ink` to `#f3f2ee` in the generated HTML | | More / fewer shots | The script generates one `
` per pair passed | | Sponsor logo in header | Embed a `` beneath `

` using the published logo URL | --- ## Activation surface prompts (ready to use) **Exhibition booth:** > "A sleek brand-activation booth — a large [BRAND] sign on the wall, two demo screens on slim stands showing clean grid interfaces, a low welcome counter, soft [ACCENT] pastel accents. No crowds." **Build Bar / communal lounge:** > "A [BRAND] Build Bar — a large [BRAND] sign overhead, communal counter with bar stools, warm pendant lights, lounge chairs and plants, soft [ACCENT] accents. A few effortlessly cool attendees in elevated casual (linen, denim) collaborate at the counter." **Registration + photo-op:** > "A [BRAND] registration moment — a large [BRAND] backdrop wall to one side, a sleek check-in desk, badge/lanyard stand. No hanging ceiling banners. The central entrance is completely unobstructed." **Keynote stage:** > "A [BRAND]-presented keynote — large LED backdrop displaying the [BRAND] logo and '[EVENT]' in the existing stage architecture. Keep all stage lighting, the podium, and audience seating. A speaker stands at the podium." --- ## Common failure modes & fixes | Failure | Fix | |---|---| | Room architecture changes | Add "Do NOT add, remove, or modify ANY architectural element — every wall, floor, column, window, plant and fixture must be unchanged" | | Logo has wrong colors | Add "Strictly [COLOR], absolutely NO [OTHER COLORS]" + pass real logo as `inputImages[1]` | | Fine print / dates appear | Add "The ONLY text anywhere is the [BRAND] wordmark. No dates, no city names, no taglines." | | Doorway blocked by booth | Add "Keep the central entrance completely unobstructed. Place all activation elements flanking the sides only." | | People look corporate | Add "NO business suits. Elevated casual only — linen, quality denim, knitwear." |
